title: Palindrome Checker
statement:
Write a Python program that reads a string from input and prints `True` if it is a palindrome, or `False` otherwise.

A palindrome is a word that reads the same forwards and backwards (case-insensitive).

Do NOT use any built-in `reversed()` function or slicing tricks — compare character by character.
starter_code:
def is_palindrome(s):
    # Write your solution here (compare char by char)
    pass

s = input().strip()
print(is_palindrome(s))
constraints: Input string will only contain alphabetic characters
Do not use slicing like s[::-1]
